title: How can we get in touch?
content: The easiest way is through our contact form or by email. We'd be happy to discuss your project, answer questions and explore how we can support your production in Spain or the Canary Islands.

title: How early should we contact you?
content: The earlier, the better. Early conversations create more opportunities for location access, incentive planning, scheduling and production optimization.

title: Do you provide full production services?
content: Yes. We support productions across all stages, including location scouting, permitting, crew sourcing, logistics, production management and local coordination.

title: Do you work with international productions?
content: Absolutely. We regularly collaborate with production companies, agencies, brands and studios from around the world filming in Spain and the Canary Islands.

title: Do you only work in the Canary Islands?
content: No. While the Canary Islands are a key production hub, we support projects throughout mainland Spain and collaborate with partners across multiple regions.

title: Can you support small productions as well as large shoots?
content: Yes. Our services scale according to each project's requirements, from lean commercial productions to large international film and television projects.

title: Can you assist with permits and filming permissions?
content: Yes. We coordinate permit applications and work closely with local authorities to help productions navigate regional filming requirements.

title: Do you provide location scouting services?
content: We offer both location research and on-the-ground scouting, helping productions identify locations that satisfy creative, logistical and budgetary requirements.

title: Can you help source equipment?
content: Through our network of trusted suppliers, we can coordinate camera, lighting, grip, transport and additional production equipment based on project needs.

title: Why do productions keep coming back to Spain?
content: Because Spain combines production infrastructure, experienced crews, diverse locations and competitive incentives within a single territory.

title: What makes the Canary Islands unique for filming?
content: Few destinations offer such a wide range of landscapes, stable weather conditions and production incentives within a relatively small geographic area.

title: Can Spain double for other countries?
content: Regularly. Productions use Spain to recreate everything from North America and the Middle East to Latin America and various European settings.

title: How early should location conversations begin?
content: Ideally during creative development. Early planning often unlocks opportunities that are no longer available later in the process.

title: How important is local knowledge?
content: Local expertise often reveals solutions, alternatives and opportunities that don't appear in databases, location libraries or scouting decks.

title: Can a location save production costs?
content: Absolutely. The right location can reduce travel, logistics, shooting days and overall production complexity.

title: What is the biggest misconception about filming in Spain?
content: That productions come only for incentives. In reality, many return because of the infrastructure, reliability and creative flexibility available throughout the country.

title: How do you balance creative ambitions with practical realities?
content: The best solutions emerge when creative and production considerations develop together rather than competing against one another.

title: Why do international productions choose local crews?
content: Local crews bring regional knowledge, operational efficiency and extensive experience working within the specific realities of each territory.

title: How important is flexibility during production?
content: Production rarely follows a perfect plan. Flexibility allows teams to adapt quickly while maintaining creative and operational goals.

title: Can one region offer multiple visual identities?
content: Often yes. Many Spanish regions contain dramatically different environments within short travel distances.

title: What makes a strong production partnership?
content: Trust, transparency and shared objectives. The most successful collaborations operate as one integrated team.

title: How do incentives influence location decisions?
content: Incentives can be an important factor, but they work best when combined with locations, infrastructure and crews that support the project's creative goals.

title: What should producers consider before choosing a destination?
content: Location diversity, logistics, crew availability, permitting requirements, infrastructure and overall production efficiency.

title: What is often overlooked during pre-production?
content: Small operational details. Early planning around access, scheduling and logistics frequently prevents larger challenges later.

title: What defines a successful production experience?
content: A process where creative teams can focus on storytelling while operational challenges are managed effectively behind the scenes.
